title: You Bet Your Life

jokes.text: Alex, the question is, 'What are three things found in Uncle Phil's beard?'
jokes.setup: Will treating Uncle Phil's eating habits like a Jeopardy answer
jokes.punchline: Implying Uncle Phil's beard is full of food debris
jokes.explanation: Clever format parody combined with character-specific visual comedy about Uncle Phil's size/eating habits

jokes.text: Yuck! Now, I lost my appetite. Thanks a lot, Will.
jokes.setup: Will's gross-out joke about Uncle Phil's beard
jokes.punchline: Carlton's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat, functional but not memorable

jokes.text: No, thank you, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton sarcastically thanking Will for ruining his appetite
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically thanking Carlton back for the reaction
jokes.explanation: Quick sarcastic comeback, economical but predictable

jokes.text: University of Nevada. Where at graduation... they don't wear their tassels on their caps, if you know what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Will describing the university
jokes.punchline: Innuendo about tassels/strippers at a party school
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with good setup, though relies on innuendo

jokes.text: You sure are lucky, Will. Spending the weekend at a college campus with 4,000 hot coeds... and you get to bunk with Dad and Sara Lee.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing Will's 'lucky' college trip
jokes.punchline: The punchline that Will has to stay with Uncle Phil (Dad) and his wife
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with ironic contrast

jokes.text: Jazz, you all right, man? You got a kind of a Clark Kent-Urkel thing jumping all over you.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on Jazz's appearance
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jazz to Clark Kent and Urkel simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Creative pop culture comparison that captures Jazz's awkward transformation

jokes.text: Actually, Will, he looks like me. Well, yeah, that's what I was saying.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinking he's defending Jazz
jokes.punchline: Will confirming that's exactly what he meant (Carlton looks nerdy too)
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character work - Carlton unwittingly insults himself

jokes.text: I spent eight years in high school.
jokes.setup: Jazz talking about being ready for college
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he took 8 years to graduate high school
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery of Jazz's academic struggles, perfectly economical

jokes.text: Here, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Jazz realizing he can't go and has to return his clothes
jokes.punchline: Jazz giving Carlton the preppy outfit since he can't use it
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the costume gag, logical character beat

jokes.text: This is a list of sororities. Yes, sir, from alpha to gamma to bad Mama-Jamma.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his college itinerary to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Playing with Greek letter names to include 'Mama-Jamma'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing Greek letters with slang, very Will-specific

jokes.text: Well, yeah, but look at you now, G.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey saying he spent university studying
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out Geoffrey's current situation as a butler
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback highlighting the irony of Geoffrey's situation

jokes.text: Touché. Did you by any chance see Murder, She Wrote last week? No. Good. Watch your back.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey acknowledging Will's point about his life
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey making a veiled threat using Murder, She Wrote reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected threat disguised as casual TV conversation - very Geoffrey

jokes.text: Look, you guys, Carlton's 18 now. I don't think we should continue to encourage that type of behavior. We're talking about the new baby.
jokes.setup: Hilary seeming to mature about Carlton
jokes.punchline: Revealing they were actually talking about the baby, not Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke playing on the confusion between Carlton and baby behavior

jokes.text: All right, but I draw the line at breastfeeding.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey being asked to help with the baby
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey setting a boundary at breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Unexpected punchline with good economy, fits Geoffrey's dry humor

jokes.text: Cough up a lung? No. It's okay. I'm fine. I never felt better. Call an ambulance.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Uncle Phil is ready to go
jokes.punchline: Phil claiming he's fine while clearly needing medical attention
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction joke, well-executed with Uncle Phil's stubbornness

jokes.text: Hey, I tell you what. Come on, you can make it. There's a Dodger dog in it for you.
jokes.setup: Will trying to convince sick Uncle Phil to go
jokes.punchline: Offering food as motivation to Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Plays on Uncle Phil's love of food, character-specific humor

jokes.text: Because I'm smarter than the average bear.
jokes.setup: Will asking why Phil can't trust him
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil using Yogi Bear reference to explain his wisdom
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Uncle Phil's fatherly but playful nature

jokes.text: Yes, sir, Deputy Dawg.
jokes.setup: Carlton being overly authoritative about driving rules
jokes.punchline: Will comparing Carlton to the cartoon deputy dog
jokes.explanation: Good cartoon reference that captures Carlton's officious nature

jokes.text: Yes, hello, this is Tony Montana. I just saw your billboard on the highway. If you know what's good for you... you'll make me a pepperoni pizza with anchovies on it.
jokes.setup: Will prank calling a pizza place
jokes.punchline: Using Scarface character to threaten over pizza order
jokes.explanation: Amusing prank call using movie character, fits Will's mischievous nature

jokes.text: Just look for the little, square-head guy who can just barely see over the steering wheel.
jokes.setup: Will giving directions on the prank call
jokes.punchline: Describing Carlton in unflattering physical terms
jokes.explanation: Visual humor about Carlton's height and appearance, character-specific

jokes.text: I want you to speed up and catch up with them girls, man. That's it, mister, you're on a time-out.
jokes.setup: Will wanting Carlton to chase girls in the car
jokes.punchline: Carlton treating Will like a child with 'time-out'
jokes.explanation: Perfect role reversal with Carlton being the authority figure

jokes.text: Look, we're young and we're single. One of us is really handsome, the other one is you.
jokes.setup: Will trying to convince Carlton to be more fun
jokes.punchline: Will insulting Carlton while complimenting himself
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured insult with great economy and quotable punchline

jokes.text: My job is to ruin your fun.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining his role representing his father
jokes.punchline: Carlton matter-of-factly stating his job is to ruin Will's fun
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery of Carlton's uptight nature, very economical

jokes.text: And you really love your work, don't you?
jokes.setup: Carlton saying his job is to ruin Will's fun
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically noting Carlton's enthusiasm for the role
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that highlights the dynamic between the cousins

jokes.text: Well, that may be so, but whether you stop or not, I'm going in one minute.
jokes.setup: Carlton's rigid bathroom schedule
jokes.punchline: Will threatening to urinate regardless of stopping
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the bathroom conflict with implied threat

jokes.text: We ain't black. We just got back from Hawaii.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton entering a sketchy establishment
jokes.punchline: Will's survival strategy of pretending they're tanned, not Black
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary disguised as survival humor, incredibly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Look, you have exactly 45 seconds to get back from the bathroom. If you're not back, I'm leaving without you.
jokes.setup: Carlton's obsessive scheduling continues
jokes.punchline: Carlton giving Will exactly 45 seconds for bathroom use
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Carlton's scheduling neurosis, escalates the absurdity

jokes.text: Carlton, it may take you 45 seconds... but it takes a fully grown man a little while longer.
jokes.setup: Carlton's 45-second bathroom limit
jokes.punchline: Will implying Carlton isn't a fully grown man
jokes.explanation: Good comeback insulting Carlton's maturity/size in context

jokes.text: Will, I think the engine's knocking. That's not where the engine is, stupid.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinking the engine is knocking
jokes.punchline: Carlton is hearing knocking from the trunk, not the engine
jokes.explanation: Setup for Jazz reveal, plays on Carlton's lack of car knowledge

jokes.text: Jazz, how did you get in the trunk? It was easy. Oh, by the way, you need a new lock for your garage.
jokes.setup: Jazz appearing from the trunk unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Jazz casually mentioning he broke into their garage
jokes.explanation: Great reveal with Jazz's casual admission of breaking and entering

jokes.text: you know, hood things.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to sound car-knowledgeable
jokes.punchline: Carlton's vague, non-specific car terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - trying to sound knowledgeable while being completely vague

jokes.text: Hey, touch that handle, Granny, and you'll be pulling back a nub.
jokes.setup: Someone about to play a slot machine Will is eyeing
jokes.punchline: Will threatening an elderly woman over a slot machine
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's immediate gambling addiction with comic threat

jokes.text: Well, considering my limited experience with foreign cars... this being the first one anyone's actually let me touch... I think it went pretty well.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining what went wrong with the expensive car
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete inexperience with the car he just worked on
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reveal of Homer's incompetence after the damage is done

jokes.text: Let me guess. You have a nuclear reactor in your backyard, don't you?
jokes.setup: Homer's incompetence with the car
jokes.punchline: Will comparing Homer to Homer Simpson
jokes.explanation: Clever Simpsons reference based on the character name and incompetence

jokes.text: It's $300. There's $25 for the gas... and $275 to call Germany to find out whether it took regular or unleaded.
jokes.setup: The outrageous bill for car service
jokes.punchline: Calling Germany internationally to ask about gas type
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation of incompetence with specific, believable details

jokes.text: Well, I prefer the term 'emotionally challenged.'
jokes.setup: Will calling Homer crazy
jokes.punchline: Homer's PC euphemism for his mental state
jokes.explanation: Good politically correct humor twist, fits the character's odd nature

jokes.text: Well, I've only got $40 cash. You've got to take a credit card. I mean, everyone takes credit cards. What kind of hick are you? The kind that don't take credit cards.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to pay with credit card and insulting Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer matter-of-factly confirming he's exactly that kind of hick
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Carlton's city boy assumptions

jokes.text: Well, between this soda can and what I got in my wallet... I'm good for a nickel.
jokes.setup: Jazz being asked how much money he has
jokes.punchline: Jazz counting a soda can as part of his wealth for a nickel total
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz poverty joke with great specificity and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: If you need me, I'll be at McDonald's watching the numbers change.
jokes.setup: Homer giving the guys time to get money
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple entertainment of watching McDonald's customer counter
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific character detail that's both absurd and believable

jokes.text: Can I get $40 in quarters?
jokes.setup: Carlton refusing to let Will gamble because they're underage
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately getting gambling money himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy moment showing Carlton's instant corruption

jokes.text: What do you say, Jazz? You turn my $30 into $300? Does James Brown use a hot comb?
jokes.setup: Will asking Jazz if he can win at gambling
jokes.punchline: Jazz's confident response using James Brown reference
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that expresses confidence while being character-appropriate

jokes.text: Jazz, that was our last chip, man. Don't worry, man. We got her right where we want her.
jokes.setup: Will panicking about losing their last chip
jokes.punchline: Jazz's delusional confidence despite clearly losing
jokes.explanation: Standard gambling confidence joke, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: Jazz, you lost all our money, man. I thought you said you knew how to play poker. Poker? You mean this isn't Go Fish?
jokes.setup: Will angry that Jazz lost at poker
jokes.punchline: Jazz thought they were playing Go Fish, not poker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reveal that perfectly explains Jazz's terrible play and fits his character

jokes.text: Come on, baby, have a heart. I'm just a poor black man trying to get to a panty raid.
jokes.setup: Will pleading with Lola for sympathy
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous appeal combining race and juvenile college goals
jokes.explanation: Amusing combination of social commentary and juvenile college humor

jokes.text: Yeah, my Studebaker broke down way back in '78. I've been working here all this time to pay it off.
jokes.setup: Lola introducing Woodrow as someone working off debt
jokes.punchline: Woodrow's been trapped there for decades over a car repair
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation showing the trap Will might fall into

jokes.text: Fifteen more years and you can just eat my dust.
jokes.setup: Woodrow trapped working for decades
jokes.punchline: Woodrow's optimistic timeline for escape in 15 more years
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing delusion mixed with false optimism

jokes.text: Check it out. Look, Lola... I don't want to die here an old white man, okay?
jokes.setup: Will seeing Woodrow's fate and panicking
jokes.punchline: Will's fear of transformation after decades of captivity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist fear that's both racial commentary and character-specific panic

jokes.text: Jazz, ain't nobody going to pay no money to see your sixth toe.
jokes.setup: Jazz suggesting he has something valuable in his sock
jokes.punchline: Will assuming it's Jazz's deformed foot
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup for Jazz's money reveal, character-appropriate assumption

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Emergency bean-pot fund.
jokes.setup: Jazz revealing hidden money
jokes.punchline: Jazz's specific terminology for his emergency money
jokes.explanation: Great character detail with specific Jazz terminology for his poverty preparations

jokes.text: Okay. I'm thinking of a number between one and ten. What is it? Four. Damn!
jokes.setup: Carlton proposing a simple guessing game
jokes.punchline: Lola immediately guessing correctly to Carlton's surprise
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Carlton's gambling naivety with economical execution

jokes.text: A black king, and I don't mean Shaka Zulu.
jokes.setup: Carlton revealing his winning card
jokes.punchline: Carlton's unnecessary clarification about card vs historical figure
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that shows Carlton's knowledge while being unnecessarily pedantic

jokes.text: Cherry, cherry, bell! Every time a cherry, cherry, bell!
jokes.setup: Carlton becoming obsessed with slot machine
jokes.punchline: Carlton's frustrated repetition of the losing combination
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Carlton's descent into gambling obsession

jokes.text: It just sits there taunting me. 'What's wrong, Carlton?' 'Lost your nerve, sissy boy?'
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining his obsession with the machine
jokes.punchline: Carlton imagining the slot machine talking to him with specific insults
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Carlton's psychology and the machine's supposed taunts

jokes.text: If I apply the Vulcan death grip, and press real hard right here... I believe I can make him black out.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to help Carlton with his obsession
jokes.punchline: Jazz's ridiculous Star Trek solution to gambling addiction
jokes.explanation: Great Jazz moment combining pop culture with his pseudo-intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: Look, look, Carlton. Listen, if you get in the car right now... I promise we can sing the theme to West Side Story, okay? You can be Maria.
jokes.setup: Will trying to lure Carlton away from gambling
jokes.punchline: Will offering Carlton the female lead in West Side Story
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Carlton's theater interests and Will's knowledge of them

jokes.text: If I just play 9,063 more times, I know I can beat it.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining his gambling strategy
jokes.punchline: Carlton's precise but ridiculous calculation for winning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - applying his analytical mind to gambling with absurd precision

jokes.text: Buzz off, flattop.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Carlton to leave
jokes.punchline: Carlton's uncharacteristic rudeness and odd insult choice
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing gambling has corrupted Carlton's usual politeness

jokes.text: Why don't you just get off my damn back?
jokes.setup: Carlton's gambling addiction escalating
jokes.punchline: Carlton swearing at Will, completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Shocking character moment - Carlton using profanity shows the depth of his corruption

jokes.text: That boy put the 'E' in ignorant.
jokes.setup: Jazz commenting on Carlton's behavior
jokes.punchline: Jazz's malapropism adding an E to 'ignorant'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz malapropism that's both funny and ironically demonstrates his own ignorance

jokes.text: all I keep thinking about is that little midget we left back there.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his distraction
jokes.punchline: Will referring to Carlton as a 'little midget'
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke about Carlton, shows Will's concern despite the insult

jokes.text: Will, honestly, I don't think Ashley wanted to come along.
jokes.setup: Will talking about the 'midget' they left behind
jokes.punchline: Jazz thinking Will means Ashley, not Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jazz's confusion and adding to Carlton height jokes

jokes.text: Then he should feel right at home.
jokes.setup: Will saying Carlton is without friends
jokes.punchline: Jazz's cruel but accurate observation about Carlton's social status
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical insult that rings true to Carlton's character situation

jokes.text: Could luck be a lady tonight? Tramp.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to get lucky at slots
jokes.punchline: Carlton calling luck a tramp when it doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment mixing high culture reference with petty insults

jokes.text: Shirley Temple. Hey, no giggling when you bring it to me.
jokes.setup: Carlton ordering a drink at the bar
jokes.punchline: Carlton ordering a child's drink and being defensive about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - childish drink choice with adult defensiveness

jokes.text: Can it, steroid breath.
jokes.setup: Carlton confronting a large man
jokes.punchline: Carlton's weak insult to an obviously dangerous person
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's complete lack of street sense and fighting ability

jokes.text: Hey, man, you tore my shirt. My mother bought me this shirt.
jokes.setup: Carlton's shirt getting torn in confrontation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's childish complaint about his mother buying his clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his childish dependency and priorities

jokes.text: Why don't you go call your mom, have her send you a new shirt?
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about his torn shirt
jokes.punchline: Bo's mocking suggestion about Carlton's mother dependency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation mocking Carlton's childish complaint

jokes.text: Well, I mean, I wasn't suggesting me specifically.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Bo pick on someone his own size
jokes.punchline: Will immediately backing down when Bo looks at him
jokes.explanation: Perfect coward moment with great timing and self-preservation instinct

jokes.text: Look, the time comes when a man has to take responsibility... and fight his own battles. And I'll let you know when the time comes.
jokes.setup: Will seeming to take a noble stance about fighting
jokes.punchline: Will immediately revealing he's not ready for that time
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character cowardice and quotable structure

jokes.text: How good are you at faking seizures?
jokes.setup: Jazz offering fighting advice to Will
jokes.punchline: Jazz's ridiculous medical emergency escape plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escape plan that's perfectly in character for Jazz

jokes.text: Just take the keys, start the engine. I'm going to bite him and jump in the car.
jokes.setup: Will planning his fighting strategy
jokes.punchline: Will's animalistic bite-and-run strategy
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist fighting plan showing Will's desperation and lack of combat skills

jokes.text: Let's get busy. I ain't got all night. Nightline is on in two minutes.
jokes.setup: Bo ready to fight
jokes.punchline: Bo's concern about missing his news program
jokes.explanation: Good character detail making the intimidating Bo more human and routine-focused

jokes.text: Anybody got any kryptonite in the house?
jokes.setup: Will seeing how big and strong Bo is
jokes.punchline: Will comparing Bo to Superman and asking for his weakness
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference showing Will's desperation with economical delivery

jokes.text: We brothers, man. This is crazy. I mean, we shouldn't be out here about to fight over a couple quarters.
jokes.setup: Will about to fight Bo
jokes.punchline: Will's appeal to racial solidarity to avoid fighting
jokes.explanation: Smart character moment showing Will's intelligence and survival instincts

jokes.text: And if you want to be the brother that casts the first stone for the degradation of the entire race... then you go right ahead.
jokes.setup: Will continuing his racial solidarity appeal
jokes.punchline: Will making Bo responsible for all racial relations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation putting enormous social pressure on Bo to back down

jokes.text: Will, for a little while, I was living on the edge. I, Carlton Banks, recording secretary of the audio visual squad... was gambling, swearing, and staying up really, really late.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing his wild experience
jokes.punchline: Carlton's incredibly tame version of 'living on the edge'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his sheltered perspective on what constitutes rebellion

jokes.text: Quick, open the back window. Yo, for what, man? I'm going to moon Nevada.
jokes.setup: Carlton wanting to continue his rebellious streak
jokes.punchline: Carlton's plan to moon an entire state
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Carlton's rebellion with absurd geographic target

jokes.text: Don't worry, Will. This is my lucky day.
jokes.setup: Carlton about to moon Nevada
jokes.punchline: Carlton's timing being terrible as police approach
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic timing joke with Carlton's bad luck

jokes.text: I knew your butt was going to get us in trouble, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting them pulled over for mooning
jokes.punchline: Will's literal reference to Carlton's butt causing trouble
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay tying together the mooning incident with getting in trouble

tmdb_overview: Heavyweight boxing champ Riddick Brown wreaks havoc with Carlton's mind and Will's face at a funky cafe-casino in Nevada, where the cousins have stopped on their way to check out a college for Will.
